2606.24162.title: BehaviorBench: Benchmarking Foundation Models for Behavioral Science Tasks
2606.24162.paper_summary: Foundation models have been increasingly applied to behavioral science domains such as psychology, sociology, and economics. While these models show promise in individual tasks such as survey response prediction and human-subject experiment simulation, there remains no systematic understanding of how well they perform across diverse behavioral science tasks, contexts, and populations. We introduce BehaviorBench, a comprehensive benchmark that evaluates foundation models along four core capabilities: (1) behavior prediction and simulation, (2) strategic decision-making, (3) subject-trait inference, and (4) behavioral knowledge application. Crucially, BehaviorBench evaluates model outputs at both the individual and distributional levels, capturing not only per-subject accuracy but also population-level alignment, an essential requirement for behavioral validity. Leveraging the tasks in BehaviorBench, we further develop Be.FM-1.5, extending the Be.FM family of behavioral foundation models fine-tuned on behavioral data. Our results reveal a considerable gap: proprietary general-purpose models excel at individual-level prediction and knowledge-intensive tasks, whereas behavioral foundation models, fine-tuned on behavioral data, achieve substantially stronger distributional alignment. Notably, Be.FM-1.5 leads on distributional metrics and remains competitive on individual-level metrics, suggesting that proper behavioral adaptation can close the gap. Our results highlight the importance of distributional evaluation, establish BehaviorBench as a foundation for developing and assessing behaviorally aligned AI systems, and demonstrate Be.FM-1.5's potential for a broad range of behavioral science studies. Our BehaviorBench and Be.FM-1.5 models can be accessed via https://umich-foreseer.github.io/behaviorbench/.
2606.24162.paper_summary_zh: 基礎模型在心理學、社會學和經濟學等行為科學領域的應用越來越廣泛。雖然這些模型在個別任務如調查回應預測和人類受試者實驗模擬中顯示出潛力，但對於它們在多樣的行為科學任務、情境和人群中的表現，仍然缺乏系統性的理解。我們介紹了BehaviorBench，一個全面的基準，評估基礎模型在四個核心能力方面的表現：(1) 行為預測和模擬，(2) 策略決策，(3) 受試者特徵推斷，以及 (4) 行為知識應用。至關重要的是，BehaviorBench在個體和分佈層面上評估模型輸出，不僅捕捉每位受試者的準確性，還包括人口層面的對齊，這是行為有效性的一個基本要求。利用BehaviorBench中的任務，我們進一步開發了Be.FM-1.5，擴展了基於行為數據微調的行為基礎模型Be.FM系列。我們的結果揭示了一個顯著的差距：專有的通用模型在個體層面的預測和知識密集型任務中表現優異，而基於行為數據微調的行為基礎模型則在分佈對齊方面達到了顯著更強的表現。值得注意的是，Be.FM-1.5在分佈指標上領先，並在個體層面指標上保持競爭力，這表明適當的行為適應可以縮小這一差距。我們的結果強調了分佈評估的重要性，確立了BehaviorBench作為開發和評估行為對齊AI系統的基礎，並展示了Be.FM-1.5在廣泛的行為科學研究中的潛力。我們的BehaviorBench和Be.FM-1.5模型可以通過 https://umich-foreseer.github.io/behaviorbench/ 訪問。
2606.24162.author: Jin Huang et.al.
2606.24162.authors: Jin Huang, Yutong Xie, Wanli Song, Xingjian Zhang, Walter Yuan, Matthew O. Jackson, Qiaozhu Mei
